name: LedgerLift Validate
LedgerLift Daily Refresh
script:
# NOT a standalone script — this is the body of a Server Script record
# (Script Type: Scheduler Event, Frequency: Daily).
# Keeps Days Outstanding current on open records without anyone editing them,
# so list view sorting/filtering by age stays truthful. Paid records are left
# untouched. Uses doc.db_set with update_modified=False so the daily refresh
# does not churn the modified timestamp or version log.

today = frappe.utils.nowdate()
rows = frappe.get_all(
    "LedgerLift Tracker",
    filters={
        "recovery_status": ["!=", "Paid"],
        "oldest_invoice_date": ["is", "set"],
    },
    fields=["name", "oldest_invoice_date", "days_outstanding"],
    limit_page_length=0,
)
for row in rows:
    days = frappe.utils.date_diff(today, row.oldest_invoice_date) or 0
    if days != row.days_outstanding:
        record = frappe.get_doc("LedgerLift Tracker", row.name)
        record.db_set("days_outstanding", days, update_modified=False)

name: LedgerLift Fetch Balance
script:
customer = frappe.form_dict.get("customer")
result = {"outstanding_amount": 0, "oldest_invoice_date": None, "invoice_count": 0}
if customer:
    gl = frappe.get_all(
        "GL Entry",
        filters={"party_type": "Customer", "party": customer, "is_cancelled": 0},
        fields=["debit", "credit"],
        limit_page_length=0,
    )
    net = sum(g.debit - g.credit for g in gl)
    result["outstanding_amount"] = net

    invs = frappe.get_all(
        "Sales Invoice",
        filters={"customer": customer, "docstatus": 1, "outstanding_amount": [">", 0]},
        fields=["outstanding_amount", "posting_date"],
        order_by="posting_date asc",
        limit_page_length=0,
    )
    result["invoice_count"] = len(invs)
    if net > 0 and invs:
        si_out = sum(i.outstanding_amount for i in invs)
        running = si_out - net
        for i in invs:
            if running >= i.outstanding_amount:
                running -= i.outstanding_amount
            else:
                result["oldest_invoice_date"] = str(i.posting_date)
                break
frappe.response["message"] = result

name: LedgerLift Avg Days
script:
rows = frappe.get_all("LedgerLift Tracker",
    filters={"recovery_status": ["!=", "Paid"]},
    fields=["days_outstanding"], limit_page_length=0)
vals = [r.days_outstanding or 0 for r in rows]
avg = int(round(sum(vals) / len(vals))) if vals else 0
frappe.response["message"] = avg

name: LedgerLift Due Followups
StockPilot Freeze Classes
script:
res = frappe.get_doc("Report", "StockPilot Categories").execute_script_report({"view": "raw"})
mp = {}
for r in res[1]:
    cc = (r.get("m") or "C") + (r.get("v") or "C")
    mp[r.get("code")] = [cc, frappe.utils.flt(r.get("a12"))]
doc = frappe.get_doc("StockPilot Settings")
doc.frozen_class_map = json.dumps(mp)
doc.frozen_on = frappe.utils.nowdate()
doc.save()
frappe.response["message"] = "%d items frozen on %s" % (len(mp), doc.frozen_on)

name: StockPilot Class Freeze Refresh
Driver Slip Validate
Driver Slip Submit
Driver Slip Bootstrap
Driver Slip Make Invoice
script:
# Driver Slip -> draft PI/SI, THE RULE: one slip = ONE document, KP leg only.
# Multi-item: one invoice line per slip row; per-row rate_inc is filled by the
# office in the grid before converting. Field template = the submitted July
# batch (PI26-00042 / SI26-00667) with the standing stock fix (update_stock=1;
# warehouse from the slip, default Main Store - VAC).
# Office-only; creates a DRAFT — the accountant reviews and submits.

utype = frappe.db.get_value("User", frappe.session.user, "user_type")
if utype != "System User":
    frappe.throw("Office users only")

slip_name = frappe.form_dict.get("slip")
if not slip_name:
    frappe.throw("slip required")
slip = frappe.get_doc("Driver Slip", slip_name)

if slip.status != "Pending Review":
    frappe.throw("Slip is already " + slip.status)
if slip.direction not in ("Purchase", "Sale"):
    frappe.throw("Slip has no direction — set From (कुठून) first")
if not slip.get("items"):
    frappe.throw("Slip has no goods rows")

wh = slip.warehouse or "Main Store - VAC"
inv_items = []
for row in slip.items:
    r = frappe.utils.flt(row.rate_inc)
    if r <= 0:
        frappe.throw("Fill Rate ₹/bag on row " + str(row.idx) + " (" + (row.item or "?") + ") in the goods grid, save, then convert")
    inv_items.append({"item_code": row.item, "qty": row.qty, "rate": r, "warehouse": wh})

KP = "Krushiyog Plant, Paraswada"
remarks = "Driver Slip " + slip.name + " · Challan " + (slip.challan_no or "?") + (" · " + slip.destination if slip.destination else "")

if slip.direction == "Purchase":
    # from KP: plain purchase, unregistered supplier -> no tax rows (matches all 200+ KP PIs)
    inv = frappe.get_doc({
        "doctype": "Purchase Invoice",
        "naming_series": "PI.YY.-.#####.",
        "company": "Vijay Agro Centre",
        "supplier": KP,
        "posting_date": slip.slip_date,
        "set_posting_time": 1,
        "due_date": slip.slip_date,
        "update_stock": 1,
        "remarks": remarks,
        "items": inv_items,
    })
else:
    # to KP: in-state inclusive GST — naming the template does NOT populate rows
    # on server-side insert (the desk does that client-side), so append the
    # template's rows explicitly; rate 0 lets each item's own tax template set
    # the split, included_in_print_rate=1 back-computes from the inclusive rate.
    inv = frappe.get_doc({
        "doctype": "Sales Invoice",
        "naming_series": "SI.YY.-.#####.",
        "company": "Vijay Agro Centre",
        "customer": KP,
        "posting_date": slip.slip_date,
        "set_posting_time": 1,
        "due_date": slip.slip_date,
        "update_stock": 1,
        "taxes_and_charges": "Output GST In-state Inclusive - VAC",
        "remarks": remarks,
        "items": inv_items,
        "taxes": [
            {"charge_type": "On Net Total", "account_head": "Output Tax CGST - VAC", "rate": 0,
             "included_in_print_rate": 1, "description": "Output Tax CGST", "cost_center": "Main - VAC - VACG"},
            {"charge_type": "On Net Total", "account_head": "Output Tax SGST - VAC", "rate": 0,
             "included_in_print_rate": 1, "description": "Output Tax SGST", "cost_center": "Main - VAC - VACG"},
        ],
    })

inv.insert()

slip.status = "Invoiced"
slip.invoice_type = inv.doctype
slip.invoice_ref = inv.name
slip.save()

frappe.response["message"] = {"invoice": inv.name, "doctype": inv.doctype, "grand_total": inv.grand_total}

name: Stock Count Bootstrap
script:
# Stock Count PWA (/count) bootstrap — today's due list, BLIND.
#
# ==========================================================================
# BLIND-COUNT CONTRACT (user decision 2026-07-23):
# This response must NEVER carry book quantity or stock value, directly or
# by implication. StockPilot Count Schedule emits book_qty / stock_value for
# the printed desk sheet — we allow-list the fields we copy out rather than
# blacklisting, so a new column in the report cannot silently leak.
# Two implicit leaks are neutralised below:
#   - class "NEG" tells the counter the book is negative -> "PRIORITY"
#   - profile "NEGATIVE STOCK - priority count" -> "Priority count"
# Any change here must keep test sc_test_staging.test_blind_contract green.
# ==========================================================================

p = frappe.form_dict
focal = p.get("date") or frappe.utils.nowdate()
req_wh = p.get("warehouse")

res = frappe.get_doc("Report", "StockPilot Count Schedule").execute_script_report(
    {"view": "Day Schedule", "date": focal})
raw = res[1]

default_wh = frappe.db.get_single_value("Stock Settings", "default_warehouse")

# A count belongs to ONE company. Derived from the data, never hardcoded:
# the default warehouse's company, falling back to the global default. On both
# sites that resolves to Vijay Agro Centre. This is what keeps Krushiyog /
# KFC / Solar locations off the counter's phone — prod has 23 countable
# warehouses across 4 companies, and all but one of them are a wrong answer.
company = None
if default_wh:
    company = frappe.db.get_value("Warehouse", default_wh, "company")
if not company:
    company = frappe.db.get_single_value("Global Defaults", "default_company")

# Countable warehouses: real storage locations of THIS company only. Group
# nodes hold no stock of their own and a disabled one must not accept a count.
whs = []
for w in frappe.get_all("Warehouse",
                        filters={"is_group": 0, "disabled": 0, "company": company},
                        fields=["name"],
                        order_by="name", limit_page_length=0):
    whs.append({"name": w.name})
    if not default_wh:
        default_wh = w.name

# a requested warehouse outside the count's company is ignored, not honoured
wh = default_wh
if req_wh:
    if frappe.db.get_value("Warehouse", req_wh, "company") == company:
        wh = req_wh

# ---- allow-listed projection of the schedule rows ------------------------
rows = []
codes = []
for r in raw:
    code = r.get("item")
    if not code:
        continue
    if code not in codes:
        codes.append(code)

    cat = r.get("cc") or ""
    prof = r.get("cdesc") or ""
    # de-leak: NEG / NEGATIVE STOCK reveal the sign of the book balance
    if cat == "NEG":
        cat = "PRIORITY"
    elif cat[0:3] == "NEG":
        cat = "PRIORITY" + cat[3:]
    if prof.find("NEGATIVE STOCK") >= 0:
        prof = prof.replace("NEGATIVE STOCK - priority count", "Priority count")

    rows.append({
        "item": code,
        "label": r.get("label") or code,
        "group": r.get("igrp") or "",
        "cat": cat,
        "profile": prof,
        "freq": frappe.utils.cint(r.get("freq")),
        "last_counted": r.get("last_counted"),
        "family": frappe.utils.cint(r.get("indent")),
        "due": frappe.utils.cint(r.get("due_flag")),
    })

# ---- stock UOM (what the counter is typing: packs, KG, Nos) --------------
uom = {}
if codes:
    for it in frappe.get_all("Item", filters={"name": ["in", codes]},
                             fields=["name", "stock_uom"], limit_page_length=0):
        uom[it.name] = it.stock_uom or ""
for row in rows:
    row["uom"] = uom.get(row["item"]) or ""

# ---- what has already been counted today, IN THIS WAREHOUSE -------------
# Two people on the same aisle must not double-count. Scoped to the warehouse
# being counted: the same item legitimately gets counted once per location.
# Item codes only — no quantities, so this stays inside the blind contract.
done = []
sess = frappe.get_all("Stock Count Session",
                      filters={"count_date": focal, "warehouse": wh,
                               "status": ["!=", "Rejected"]},
                      pluck="name", limit_page_length=0)
if sess:
    # NB: no `parent=` kwarg. That is the REST-layer spelling
    # (frappe.client.get_list maps it onto parent_doctype); in-process,
    # DatabaseQuery.execute() has no `parent` parameter and no **kwargs, so
    # passing it raises TypeError and 500s the whole bootstrap. The filters
    # below scope the query on their own — same shape as the deployed
    # "Driver Slip Bootstrap" script.
    for e in frappe.get_all("Stock Count Entry",
                            filters={"parent": ["in", sess],
                                     "parenttype": "Stock Count Session"},
                            fields=["item"], limit_page_length=0):
        if e.item and e.item not in done:
            done.append(e.item)

# ---- searchable master for ad-hoc counts --------------------------------
items = frappe.get_all("Item",
                       filters={"disabled": 0, "is_stock_item": 1},
                       fields=["name", "item_name", "stock_uom"],
                       limit_page_length=0, order_by="name")

frappe.response["message"] = {
    "counter": frappe.db.get_value("User", frappe.session.user, "full_name"),
    "is_office": frappe.db.get_value("User", frappe.session.user, "user_type") == "System User",
    "date": focal,
    "server_date": frappe.utils.nowdate(),
    "server_time": frappe.utils.nowtime(),
    "company": company,
    "warehouse": wh,
    "warehouses": whs,
    "rows": rows,
    "counted_today": done,
    "items": items,
}

name: Stock Count Submit
script:
# Stock Count PWA (/count) submit — append counted lines to a Count Session.
#
# Idempotent at TWO levels, because a phone retries whole batches:
#   session_uid -> one Stock Count Session per counting run (per owner)
#   line uid    -> one Stock Count Entry per counted item, replays are no-ops
# (proven on the /slip build: a prod bench 502'd mid-submit and the same-uid
# retry created exactly one doc.)
#
# The BOOK SNAPSHOT is taken here, server-side, and is never sent to or
# accepted from the phone (blind-count contract). It is read AS OF THE COUNT
# DATETIME from the Stock Ledger — NOT from tabBin — because an offline phone
# may sync hours after the shelf was actually counted, by which time the live
# Bin no longer reflects what was on the books when the counter looked.

p = frappe.form_dict

suid = (p.get("session_uid") or "").strip()
if not suid:
    frappe.throw("session_uid required")

lines = p.get("lines") or []
if isinstance(lines, str):
    # json.loads, NOT frappe.parse_json — parse_json is absent from this
    # site's safe_exec globals (verified: AttributeError on staging). The
    # deployed "Driver Slip Submit" fixture calls parse_json on the same
    # branch and only escapes it because its client always posts an array,
    # never a JSON string.
    lines = json.loads(lines)

# "Finish" is a legitimate status-only call: the phone may have synced every
# line already and just be closing the run. It may NOT conjure an empty session
# out of an unknown uid, though.
finish = frappe.utils.cint(p.get("finish"))
if not lines and not finish:
    frappe.throw("no lines")

wh = frappe.db.get_single_value("Stock Settings", "default_warehouse")

# The counter picks the location on the phone, so it is untrusted input: a
# group node or a disabled warehouse would build a Stock Reconciliation that
# can never be submitted, and an unknown name would silently create stock
# nowhere. Validate before it reaches the session.
# the count's company, derived the same way as in the bootstrap
company = None
if wh:
    company = frappe.db.get_value("Warehouse", wh, "company")
if not company:
    company = frappe.db.get_single_value("Global Defaults", "default_company")

req_wh = p.get("warehouse")
if req_wh:
    info = frappe.db.get_value("Warehouse", req_wh,
                               ["name", "is_group", "disabled", "company"], as_dict=1)
    if not info:
        frappe.throw("Unknown warehouse: " + str(req_wh))
    if info.is_group:
        frappe.throw(str(req_wh) + " is a warehouse group — pick a real location")
    if info.disabled:
        frappe.throw(str(req_wh) + " is disabled")
    # A cross-company warehouse would post a stock correction into another
    # firm's books. The phone is only ever offered this company's locations,
    # so anything else is a crafted or stale request — refuse it.
    if company and info.company != company:
        frappe.throw(str(req_wh) + " belongs to " + str(info.company) +
                     " — this count is for " + str(company))
    wh = req_wh
if not wh:
    frappe.throw("No warehouse given and no default set in Stock Settings")


def book_as_of(code, warehouse, on_date, on_time):
    """Qty + valuation on the books at the moment of counting."""
    r = frappe.db.sql(
        "select qty_after_transaction as q, valuation_rate as vr "
        "from `tabStock Ledger Entry` "
        "where item_code = %s and warehouse = %s and is_cancelled = 0 "
        "and (posting_date < %s or (posting_date = %s and posting_time <= %s)) "
        "order by posting_date desc, posting_time desc, creation desc limit 1",
        (code, warehouse, on_date, on_date, on_time), as_dict=1)
    if r:
        return [frappe.utils.flt(r[0].q), frappe.utils.flt(r[0].vr)]
    return [0.0, 0.0]


def parse_at(raw, d_date, d_time):
    """Normalise a client timestamp to [date, time].

    The phone must send LOCAL wall-clock time — "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS", or the
    same with a "T". A UTC marker is REJECTED rather than accepted: the site
    runs Asia/Kolkata, so silently taking a "Z" timestamp would shift every
    count by 5h30m, read the book at the wrong moment, and around midnight
    file the run under the wrong posting date.
    """
    s = (raw or "").strip()
    if not s:
        return [d_date, d_time]
    if s[-1:] == "Z" or s.find("+") >= 10:
        frappe.throw("Timestamp '" + s + "' carries a timezone. Send local "
                     "wall-clock time as YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.")
    d = s[0:10]
    if len(d) != 10 or d[4:5] != "-" or d[7:8] != "-":
        return [d_date, d_time]
    t = s[11:19]
    if len(t) < 8:
        t = d_time
    return [d, t]


# ---- when was this actually counted? ------------------------------------
# NOT the sync clock. An offline phone can drain hours after the shelf was
# counted — or after midnight, which would file the whole run under the wrong
# posting date. The session's moment is the EARLIEST line it has seen, and a
# later batch carrying an earlier line pulls it back (below).
earliest = ""
for ln in lines:
    a = parse_at(ln.get("at"), "", "")
    if a[0]:
        stamp = a[0] + " " + a[1]
        if not earliest or stamp < earliest:
            earliest = stamp

# ---- find or create this counting run -----------------------------------
cdate = p.get("count_date")
ctime = p.get("count_time")
if not cdate:
    if earliest:
        cdate = earliest[0:10]
    else:
        cdate = frappe.utils.nowdate()
if not ctime:
    if earliest:
        ctime = earliest[11:19]
    else:
        ctime = frappe.utils.nowtime()

name = frappe.db.get_value("Stock Count Session",
                           {"client_uid": suid, "owner": frappe.session.user},
                           "name")
if name:
    doc = frappe.get_doc("Stock Count Session", name)
else:
    if not lines:
        frappe.throw("No counting run found for this device — nothing to finish.")
    doc = frappe.get_doc({
        "doctype": "Stock Count Session",
        "client_uid": suid,
        "count_date": cdate,
        "count_time": ctime,
        "schedule_date": p.get("schedule_date") or cdate,
        "warehouse": wh,
        "source": p.get("source") or "Day Schedule",
        "status": "In Progress",
    })
    doc.insert()

if doc.status in ("Reconciled", "Rejected"):
    frappe.throw("Session " + doc.name + " is already " + doc.status)

# One session counts ONE location, because it becomes one Stock Reconciliation
# against one warehouse. The phone keys its session id on (date, warehouse) so
# this should never fire — but a mismatch would silently file a count against
# the wrong location's book, so refuse rather than guess.
if doc.warehouse and doc.warehouse != wh:
    frappe.throw("Session " + doc.name + " is counting " + doc.warehouse +
                 " — start a new run to count " + wh)

# ---- append only lines we have not already stored -----------------------
have = {}
for row in (doc.get("items") or []):
    if row.line_uid:
        have[row.line_uid] = 1

added = 0
dupes = 0
for ln in lines:
    lu = (ln.get("uid") or "").strip()
    if not lu:
        frappe.throw("every line needs a uid")
    if lu in have:
        dupes = dupes + 1
        continue
    code = ln.get("item")
    if not code:
        continue

    at = parse_at(ln.get("at"), cdate, ctime)
    on_date = at[0]
    on_time = at[1]

    snap = book_as_of(code, doc.warehouse or wh, on_date, on_time)
    cq = frappe.utils.flt(ln.get("qty"))
    dq = cq - snap[0]

    doc.append("items", {
        "item": code,
        "counted_qty": cq,
        "uom": frappe.db.get_value("Item", code, "stock_uom"),
        "book_qty": snap[0],
        "diff_qty": dq,
        "valuation_rate": snap[1],
        "diff_value": dq * snap[1],
        "counted_at": on_date + " " + on_time,
        "note": ln.get("note") or "",
        "line_uid": lu,
    })
    have[lu] = 1
    added = added + 1

# A batch that syncs later may still contain an EARLIER line (offline queue
# drained out of order). The session's posting moment must follow the count,
# so pull it back rather than leaving it at whatever the first sync saw.
if earliest:
    cur = str(doc.count_date or "") + " " + str(doc.count_time or "")
    if not doc.count_date or earliest < cur:
        doc.count_date = earliest[0:10]
        doc.count_time = earliest[11:19]

if finish:
    doc.status = "Pending Review"

doc.save()

frappe.response["message"] = {
    "session": doc.name,
    "added": added,
    "duplicates": dupes,
    "total": len(doc.get("items") or []),
    "status": doc.status,
}

name: Stock Count Make Recon
script:
# Stock Count Session -> DRAFT Stock Reconciliation. Office only; never submits.
#
# Only lines with a REAL difference go into the reconciliation. Zero-variance
# lines stay in the session as the evidence that the item was counted — they
# cannot live in the Stock Reconciliation, because ERPNext's
# remove_items_with_no_change() deletes any row whose qty AND valuation match
# the book. (Verified on prod: of 569 submitted SR rows only 12 had
# qty == current_qty, and every one of those survived on a VALUATION
# difference, never on a pure quantity match.)
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# WHY THE POSTED QTY IS NOT ALWAYS THE COUNTED QTY
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Each line's book snapshot was taken at the moment THAT shelf was counted.
# A Stock Reconciliation, however, forces an ABSOLUTE balance at ONE posting
# instant, and ERPNext then reposts every later movement on top of it. Post a
# line counted at 11:30 against a 09:00 timestamp and the sales made between
# 09:00 and 11:30 are subtracted a second time — silent phantom shrinkage,
# and with allow_negative_stock=1 nothing complains.
#
# So each counted figure is rolled forward to the session's posting instant:
#
#     qty_posted = counted + book(posting_instant) - book(counted_at)
#
# After the repost, the balance lands exactly on what was physically counted.
# When nothing moved during the count the two terms cancel and qty_posted is
# simply the counted figure — the ordinary case. The RAW counted number is
# always preserved on the session; the reconciliation is the derived
# correction document.

utype = frappe.db.get_value("User", frappe.session.user, "user_type")
if utype != "System User":
    frappe.throw("Office users only")

sname = frappe.form_dict.get("session")
if not sname:
    frappe.throw("session required")

doc = frappe.get_doc("Stock Count Session", sname)

if doc.status == "Reconciled":
    frappe.throw("Already reconciled: " + (doc.recon_ref or "?"))
if doc.status == "Rejected":
    frappe.throw("Session " + doc.name + " is Rejected")
if not doc.get("items"):
    frappe.throw("Session has no counted lines")

# ---- one line per item -----------------------------------------------------
# ERPNext keys Stock Reconciliation rows on [item_code, warehouse] and raises
# "Same item and warehouse combination already entered" during insert, naming a
# row index rather than the item. Worse, if only one of the two lines carries a
# variance no exception fires at all and the other count is silently discarded.
# Whether a re-count should SUM (same item found on two shelves) or REPLACE
# (a correction) is a business decision — refuse and let the office say which.
seen = {}
for row in doc.get("items"):
    seen[row.item] = frappe.utils.cint(seen.get(row.item)) + 1
dups = []
for k in seen:
    if seen[k] > 1:
        dups.append(k)
if dups:
    frappe.throw("Counted more than once in this session: " + ", ".join(dups) +
                 ". Merge or remove the duplicate lines before reconciling.")

wh = doc.warehouse or frappe.db.get_single_value("Stock Settings", "default_warehouse")
comp = frappe.db.get_value("Warehouse", wh, "company")
if not comp:
    frappe.throw("Warehouse " + str(wh) + " has no company")

cc = frappe.db.get_value("Company", comp, "cost_center")

# Company.stock_adjustment_account is BLANK on production for Vijay Agro Centre
# (it is set on staging and on KP/KFC/VSS) — so a staging rehearsal passes and
# every production call would fail. Fall back to the account that historical
# reconciliations actually used before giving up.
exp = frappe.db.get_value("Company", comp, "stock_adjustment_account")
if not exp:
    exp = frappe.db.get_value("Account",
                              {"company": comp, "is_group": 0, "disabled": 0,
                               "account_name": ["like", "%Stock Adjustment%"]},
                              "name")
if not exp:
    frappe.throw("Company " + comp + " has no Stock Adjustment Account, and no "
                 "account named like 'Stock Adjustment' exists for it. Set "
                 "Company > Stock Adjustment Account, then try again.")


def book_at(code, warehouse, on_date, on_time):
    """Qty on the books at a given instant (same source as the snapshot)."""
    r = frappe.db.sql(
        "select qty_after_transaction as q from `tabStock Ledger Entry` "
        "where item_code = %s and warehouse = %s and is_cancelled = 0 "
        "and (posting_date < %s or (posting_date = %s and posting_time <= %s)) "
        "order by posting_date desc, posting_time desc, creation desc limit 1",
        (code, warehouse, on_date, on_date, on_time), as_dict=1)
    if r:
        return frappe.utils.flt(r[0].q)
    return 0.0


def fallback_rate(code):
    """A valuation rate for an item the book cannot price.

    An item with no positive stock has no moving-average rate to inherit, and
    ERPNext throws 'Valuation Rate required for Item'. 132 of 729 non-zero bins
    are negative on prod, and a handful carry qty > 0 with rate 0, so this is a
    routine path rather than an edge case.
    """
    r = frappe.db.sql(
        "select pii.valuation_rate as vr, pii.rate as rt "
        "from `tabPurchase Invoice Item` pii "
        "join `tabPurchase Invoice` pi on pi.name = pii.parent "
        "where pi.docstatus = 1 and pii.item_code = %s "
        "order by pi.posting_date desc, pi.creation desc limit 1",
        (code,), as_dict=1)
    if r:
        v = frappe.utils.flt(r[0].vr)
        if v <= 0:
            v = frappe.utils.flt(r[0].rt)
        if v > 0:
            return v
    v = frappe.utils.flt(frappe.db.get_value("Item", code, "valuation_rate"))
    if v > 0:
        return v
    r2 = frappe.db.sql(
        "select sri.valuation_rate as vr "
        "from `tabStock Reconciliation Item` sri "
        "join `tabStock Reconciliation` sr on sr.name = sri.parent "
        "where sr.docstatus = 1 and sri.item_code = %s and sri.valuation_rate > 0 "
        "order by sr.posting_date desc limit 1",
        (code,), as_dict=1)
    if r2:
        return frappe.utils.flt(r2[0].vr)
    return 0.0


# ---- which lines actually differ, and when do we post? ---------------------
included = []
matched = 0
for row in doc.get("items"):
    if abs(frappe.utils.flt(row.diff_qty)) < 0.0005:
        matched = matched + 1
    else:
        included.append(row)

if not included:
    doc.status = "Reconciled"
    doc.save()
    frappe.response["message"] = {
        "recon": None, "lines": 0, "matched": matched,
        "note": "Every counted line matched the book — nothing to reconcile. "
                "The count is recorded in this session.",
    }
else:
    # Post at the LAST moment counted among the lines we are correcting: every
    # roll-forward is then a forward roll within the session, and the backdate
    # (and the repost it triggers) is as short as it can be.
    t_post = ""
    for row in included:
        if row.counted_at and str(row.counted_at) > t_post:
            t_post = str(row.counted_at)
    if not t_post:
        t_post = str(doc.count_date) + " " + str(doc.count_time or "00:00:00")
    post_date = t_post[0:10]
    post_time = t_post[11:19]
    if len(post_time) < 8:
        post_time = "00:00:00"

    rows = []
    unpriced = []
    rolled = 0
    for row in included:
        cq = frappe.utils.flt(row.counted_qty)
        bq = frappe.utils.flt(row.book_qty)
        moved = book_at(row.item, wh, post_date, post_time) - bq
        qty_posted = cq + moved
        if abs(moved) > 0.0005:
            rolled = rolled + 1

        entry = {"item_code": row.item, "warehouse": wh, "qty": qty_posted}

        # Gate the fallback on the PRICE, not the quantity: an item can sit at
        # qty > 0 with valuation_rate 0 (4 such bins on prod). Without a rate
        # the draft inserts fine and then dies on submit with "Valuation Rate
        # required", leaving the session already flipped to Reconciled.
        if cq > 0 and (bq <= 0 or frappe.utils.flt(row.valuation_rate) <= 0):
            vr = frappe.utils.flt(row.valuation_rate)
            if vr <= 0:
                vr = fallback_rate(row.item)
            if vr > 0:
                entry["valuation_rate"] = frappe.utils.flt(vr, 2)
            else:
                unpriced.append(row.item)
        rows.append(entry)

    # Hard stop BEFORE anything is inserted and before the session status moves:
    # a half-built draft that can never be submitted is worse than no draft.
    if unpriced:
        frappe.throw(
            "No valuation rate can be derived for: " + ", ".join(unpriced) +
            ". Set Valuation Rate on the Item master (or record a purchase), "
            "then make the reconciliation again.")

    sr = frappe.get_doc({
        "doctype": "Stock Reconciliation",
        "purpose": "Stock Reconciliation",
        "company": comp,
        "posting_date": post_date,
        "posting_time": post_time,
        "set_posting_time": 1,
        "expense_account": exp,
        "cost_center": cc,
        "remarks": "Stock Count Session " + doc.name + " · counted by " +
                   (doc.counted_by or doc.owner or "?") +
                   " · posted at the last counted moment " + t_post,
        "items": rows,
    })
    sr.insert()

    doc.status = "Reconciled"
    doc.recon_ref = sr.name
    doc.save()

    frappe.response["message"] = {
        "recon": sr.name,
        "lines": len(rows),
        "matched": matched,
        "rolled_forward": rolled,
        "posted_at": t_post,
        "difference_amount": frappe.utils.flt(sr.difference_amount),
    }

name: Stock Count Validate
script:
# Stock Count Session — validate. Recomputes the derived columns so a desk
# edit (office correcting a mis-keyed count) stays consistent with what the
# PWA stored.
#
# It deliberately does NOT re-snapshot book_qty / valuation_rate: those are
# the position AS OF THE COUNT, written once by stock_count_submit. Refreshing
# them here would silently rewrite history every time the form is opened and
# destroy the variance the count exists to measure.

tot = 0
var = 0
vval = 0.0

for row in (doc.get("items") or []):
    tot = tot + 1
    dq = frappe.utils.flt(row.counted_qty) - frappe.utils.flt(row.book_qty)
    row.diff_qty = dq
    row.diff_value = dq * frappe.utils.flt(row.valuation_rate)
    if abs(dq) > 0.0005:
        var = var + 1
        vval = vval + row.diff_value

doc.lines_counted = tot
doc.lines_variance = var
doc.variance_value = vval

if not doc.counted_by:
    doc.counted_by = doc.owner
